steps.title: Set up the equal-ratio equation
steps.content:
Since both rectangles have the same length-to-width ratio:
$$\frac{15}{w} = \frac{20}{x}$$
where $x$ is the width of rectangle B.

steps.title: Solve by cross-multiplication
steps.content:
Cross multiply:
$$15x = 20w$$
Divide both sides by 15:
$$x = \frac{20w}{15} = \frac{4w}{3}$$
The width of rectangle B is $\frac{4}{3}w$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
